violations.text: The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Menu lacks an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024
The hand-washing sinks in the bathroom and kitchen were lacking paper towels. PIC replaced. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink near the ice cream area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed utensils. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Raw animal foods (such as raw beef and chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as produce) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink. PIC rewashed/sanitized sheet trays. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Chicken was observed held at 120 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Mashed potatoes were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Per interview with PIC, seasoned flour that comes in contact with raw chicken is stored in a container for several days at room temperature. PIC informed to discard flour every 4 hours. PIC discarded flour and washed/sanitized container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Inspector observed a rag used with degreaser chemical stored on the deli prep unit cutting board. PIC informed, removed rag, and washed/sanitized cutting board. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by large droppings in the basement area, back hallway, and 3 bay sink area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The door to the outside, located in the back hallway, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Inspector observed condensate from the walk-in refrigeration unit dripping onto food boxes below. PIC relocated boxes. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Various items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Inspector observed staff conducting food prep (sifting flour in a large container) on the floor. PIC informed to end practice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC emptied and washed/sanitized utensils. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Cardboard is used in the construction of shelf liner under table area and blanket is used on top of freezer chest (also a prep area).Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Two level hot hold unit was observed in poor repair. Upper level was observed with an ambient temperature of 123F. Upper level was taken out of service during inspection with "out of order sign". Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Inspector observed 3 bay sink and drain board with raw chicken and flour. PIC washed/sanitized 3 bay sink during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Inspector observed drainboard not sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - hot hold unit - shelf under food prep table - prep unit New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026
Inspector observed large container with raw chicken, draining into bowl (overflowing) in the walk-in cooler. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The following areas were observed in poor repair: - hole in wall tile (3 bay sink area) - wall tiles pulling away from wall (3 bay sink area) - outlets coming out of wall (3 bay sink area AND dessert cooler area) - ceiling tiles with pervious water damage - condensate drip in walk-in cooler - self closing hinge (bathroom) New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility (including walk-in cooler and basement) have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site Certified Food Protection Manager registered with RIDOH at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
Utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
Raw chicken was observed stored in plastic beverage crates in the walk-in cooler. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit, walk-in cooler fan guard, and microwave have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
The hand sinks were observed in poor repair. Hand sink at service area was lacking handles. The hand sink in kitchen was observed with a leak. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024
The condensate from the walk-in refrigeration unit is dripping onto food boxes below. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2024
The door to the employee toilet room is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
